2020-02-25.title: ​In Coronavirus Crisis, Korean City Tries Openness, a Contrast to China
2020-02-25.abstract: Even in the center of the outbreak in Daegu, officials are not restricting the movement of people, which could be a template as the virus moves around the globe.

2020-02-25.title: Trump Sees Commitment to Religious Freedom in India as Riots Break Out
2020-02-25.abstract: The president stressed the strength of his relationship with Prime Minister Narendra Modi in meetings long on symbolism and short on concrete agreements.

2020-02-26.title: In U.S. and Germany, Community Transmission Is Now Suspected
2020-02-26.abstract: Three cases have emerged that suggest the possible beginning of a worrisome trend.

2020-02-26.title: Israel’s ‘Zombie’ Voters Stagger to a Third Election, but Figure on a Fourth
2020-02-26.abstract: Their malady has been given a name: ‘acquired political helplessness.’

2020-02-26.title: Syrian Children Freeze to Death. Bombs Rain Down. And ‘Nobody Cares.’
2020-02-26.abstract: The Syrian government’s assault on a rebel-held province has created one of the worst humanitarian emergencies of a brutal nine-year war.

2020-02-26.title: 2 Americans, 19 and 20, Go on Trial Over Killing of Italian Police Officer
2020-02-26.abstract: With defense lawyers and prosecutors giving widely differing accounts of the night in question, a jury will weigh the evidence over the killing of a paramilitary officer in Rome by a pair of teenagers.

2020-02-26.title: German Court Overturns Ban on Assisted Suicide
2020-02-26.abstract: The decision came after a group of doctors, patients and proponents sued, arguing that a 2015 law infringed on their constitutional right to make decisions about their own lives.

2020-02-27.title: Cases Soar in Italy, Iran and South Korea as Alarm Grows
2020-02-27.abstract: Financial markets were pummeled by the news. A whistle-blower complaint said that federal health workers were sent into quarantine areas unprotected and unprepared.

2020-02-27.title: Scarred and Weary, an Afghan Force Wonders: What Is Peace?
2020-02-27.abstract: A truce is prompting introspection. “Who is it on the other side?” mused a 15-year veteran of intimate warfare. “They are not even from a different district.”

2020-02-27.title: Trump’s Plan Backs Israeli Settlements. So Why Are Settlers Unhappy?
2020-02-27.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu expected the U.S. peace plan would gain him votes in Monday’s election. It might not work out that way.

2020-02-27.title: Terrorism Threat in West Africa Soars as U.S. Weighs Troop Cuts
2020-02-27.abstract: The Pentagon and the State Department are sending mixed messages about reducing America’s presence in a region bloodied by terrorist violence.

2020-02-27.title: Most Coronavirus Cases Are Mild. That’s Good and Bad News.
2020-02-27.abstract: More than 80 percent of confirmed coronavirus cases are not severe, according to a large Chinese study. But mild symptoms could also make the epidemic harder to contain.

2020-02-28.title: In Syria and Libya, Trump Is Torn Over 2 Wars, and 2 Strongmen
2020-02-28.abstract: After an airstrike on Turkish forces by Russia or its Syrian allies, President Trump may be forced to pick between Moscow and Ankara.

2020-02-28.title: Has Australia Imagined its Relaxed Identity?
2020-02-28.abstract: Wowser Nation’s art forces Australia to question its ‘nanny state’ ways — if anyone notices.

2020-02-28.title: Jimmy Lai, Hong Kong Media Baron, Is Arrested Over Role in Protests
2020-02-28.abstract: The arrest of the media tycoon is another blow to Hong Kong’s independent media as the city deals with the aftermath of mass protests and the coronavirus outbreak.

2020-02-28.title: New Unexplained Cases Reported in Oregon and California
2020-02-28.abstract: Officials in both states raced to contact people who might have been exposed to the coronavirus, and experts warned that the cases could indicate signs of spread within the United States.

2020-02-28.title: He Drove Her to the Hospital. She Gave Him the Coronavirus.
2020-02-28.abstract: Taxi and bus drivers are on the forefront of the spread of the coronavirus in Asia, even as a tourism downturn hurts their business.

2020-02-29.title: M.I.T. Researchers Cast Doubt on Bolivian Election Fraud
2020-02-29.abstract: An international audit said officials had engaged in lies and manipulation to assure victory for Evo Morales. Academics at M.I.T.’s Election Data and Science Lab questioned that finding.

2020-02-29.title: Trump Calls for Calm on Virus and Expands Travel Restrictions
2020-02-29.abstract: The announcement included elevated warnings against travel to specific regions in Italy and South Korea, and came after the U.S. recorded its first coronavirus death: a person near Seattle.

2020-02-29.title: The Driving Force in Irish Politics? Finding a Decent Place to Live
2020-02-29.abstract: A severe housing crisis has made life unbearable for many young people, who turned in droves to the previously toxic Sinn Fein party in this month’s election.

2020-02-29.title: Taliban and U.S. Strike Deal to Withdraw American Troops From Afghanistan
2020-02-29.abstract: After more than a year of talks, the agreement lays out the beginning of the end of the United States’ longest war. But many obstacles remain.

2020-02-29.title: For Seoul’s Poor, Class Strife in ‘Parasite’ Is Daily Reality
2020-02-29.abstract: Like the family in the Oscar-winning film, many in Seoul’s so-called dirt-spoon class dwell in basements far below the rich. “Those living up there must look down on people like me like pigs.”
